_comment: Policy for the REAL @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem. Tool names here are the server's actual tools (verified via tools/list). Reads are redacted so PII on disk never reaches the model; writes/edits/moves need a human to sign off; listing and search are allowed; everything else is default-deny.

read_text_file.reason: file contents may contain PII; strip before the model sees them

read_file.reason: file contents may contain PII; strip before the model sees them

read_multiple_files.reason: file contents may contain PII; strip before the model sees them

read_media_file.reason: binary media, no text PII to scrub

write_file.reason: writing to disk is a real mutation; a human must sign off

edit_file.reason: editing a file on disk is a real mutation; a human must sign off

move_file.reason: moving/renaming a file is a real mutation; a human must sign off

_taint_note: Taint tracking is intentionally OFF here: on a filesystem every read looks alike, so tainting on read would block normal read-then-edit workflows. Taint is meant for an UNTRUSTED source (e.g. web.fetch) and is demonstrated in demo/attack_scenario.py. To enable it here, add e.g. "taint_sources": ["read_text_file"] and "taint_sinks": ["write_file"].
